title: When and How to Ask for Referrals Quiz

questions.question: When is the ideal 'Satisfaction Milestone' to ask a customer for a referral?

options.text: Immediately after they pay the invoice for the first time.
When they've achieved a documented result or ROI with your product.
When they submit a support ticket about a bug.
Right before you launch a major pricing increase.

questions.explanation: Referral asks are most effective when the customer is feeling a high sense of achievement and can clearly articulate the value they've received.

questions.question: What is the core idea behind the 'Partner Ask' framing?

options.text: Asking the customer for a personal favor to help you stay in business.
Inviting the customer to join you in a mission to help others solve the same problem they faced.
Offering to make the customer a co-founder of your company.
Demanding a referral as a condition of continued service.

questions.explanation: The Partner Ask moves the referral from a 'transactional favor' to a 'helpful service,' making the customer feel like an expert and an advocate.

questions.question: According to the Airbnb case study, why did focusing on the 'Gift' to the friend increase referrals by 25%?

options.text: Because people are inherently selfish and wanted the friend to pay for them.
Because the social reward of being generous and helpful to a friend is often more powerful than a small cash kickback.
Because friends were more likely to ignore the email if it only mentioned the referrer's benefit.
Because it was easier to explain in the email subject line.

questions.explanation: Many referrers are motivated by 'Social Currency'—the desire to be seen as helpful and knowledgeable. Focusing on the benefit to the friend leverages this motivation.

questions.question: What is the best way to handle a customer who stays silent after a referral request?

options.text: Follow up every 3 days until they respond.
Send one gentle follow-up, then respect the silence and move on.
Call them on their personal phone to explain why you need the lead.
Threaten to remove them from your referral program.

questions.explanation: Referrals are high-trust, voluntary actions. Pushing a customer who isn't ready can damage the relationship and your reputation.

questions.question: What should a 'copy-paste blurb' include to reduce friction for the referrer?

options.text: A 5-paragraph history of your company.
A technical specification of your latest API update.
A short, conversational recommendation and a unique referral link.
A list of all your competitors and why you are better.

questions.explanation: The goal of the blurb is to make it brainless for the customer to share your product. Short and conversational is always better.

questions.question: Identify your most successful current customer. If you were reaching out to them today, how would you frame the 'mission' of your company to make them feel like a partner in a shared goal? Write the first two sentences of that email.
questions.promptForAI: The user should define a mission-driven framing that elevates the referral request. Evaluate the response for its ability to move beyond 'asking for a favor' and toward a 'shared goal' narrative.
